message:
test(protocol_recovery): [HIGH] regression test for ft-7axpo jitter randomization

Adds two complementary regression tests to pin the ft-7axpo fix
(commit fdd1e817 replaced deterministic sin()-derived jitter with
`rand::rng().random_range`):

1. `delay_for_attempt_jitter_is_randomized_across_calls`: samples 256
   delays at the SAME attempt index with jitter_fraction=0.3 and
   asserts ≥10 distinct millisecond values. The pre-fix sin() jitter
   would have produced exactly 1. Also asserts each sample stays
   within the jitter band capped_ms * (1 ± jitter_fraction).

2. `delay_for_attempt_zero_jitter_is_deterministic`: guarantees the
   `if jitter_range > 0.0` fast-path is preserved — callers that set
   `jitter_fraction: 0.0` (including the existing delay_for_attempt_*
   tests) still get bit-exact determinism.

Closes the ft-7axpo regression loop: without these, a future refactor
could reintroduce deterministic jitter and the original thundering-herd
hazard would resurface silently (engines retrying in lockstep after a
fleet-wide mux restart).

Verified: `cargo check --lib --no-default-features --features
asupersync-runtime` passes clean on frankenterm-core. Full test build
is blocked by unrelated compile errors in diagnostic.rs +
workflows/handlers.rs from concurrent agent work (not touched here).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
